From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former Mexican professional baseball team

| Alijadores de Tampico | |
| --- | --- |
| Information | |
| League | [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League") |
| [Ballpark](/wiki/Ballpark "Ballpark") | Parque Alijadores |
| Established | 1937 |
| Folded | 1985 |
| League championships | 1945, 1946, 1975 |
| Former name | Estibadores de Tampico (1971–72) Astros de Tamaulipas (1983–85) |
| Colors | Royal blue and gold |

The **Alijadores de Tampico** (Tampico Lightermen) were a professional baseball club based in [Tampico](/wiki/Tampico "Tampico"), [Tamaulipas](/wiki/Tamaulipas "Tamaulipas") that played in the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League") between the 1940s and 1980s.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alijadores_de_Tampico&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Alijadores de Tampico were established in 1937 and joined the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League"). The team won titles in 1945 and [1946](/wiki/1946_Mexican_League_season "1946 Mexican League season"). However, they were not very profitable and did not play from 1949 through 1970.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Between 1968 and 1970, a new stadium was built in Tampico, financed by Nicolás Canavati, a businessman from [Monterrey](/wiki/Monterrey "Monterrey"): the Parque Alijadores. In 1971 the league expanded from 10 to 12 teams, reinstating the franchise, that played as the Estibadores de Tampico (Tampico Stevedores) during two seasons. Starting 1973, the team returned to its former name and won the championship title in 1975.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

After the 1979 season, Tampico was once again removed from the league, with the franchise moving to [Toluca](/wiki/Toluca "Toluca"), becoming the [Osos Negros de Toluca](/wiki/Osos_Negros_de_Toluca "Osos Negros de Toluca"). The franchise once again returned in 1983 and was renamed the **Astros de Tampico** for that season, playing again as the Alijadores in 1984. Since then, no other team based in Tampico has participated in Mexican professional baseball.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Notable players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alijadores_de_Tampico&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable players")]

* **21** [![Mexico](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fc/Flag_of_Mexico.svg/40px-Flag_of_Mexico.svg.png)](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") All-time Mexican League great [Héctor Espino](/wiki/H%C3%A9ctor_Espino "Héctor Espino") helped win a Mexican League championship for the Alijadores de Tampico in 1975.
